threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' and don’t know the secret bus routes that locals swear by during strikes.
Last night on the 196, a guy was narrating his life story to no one in particular. It was intense—like a one-man show on wheels. Quite the ride, I must say.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the U-Bahn was packed with a mix of people singing loudly, while the busker in the corner played 'Wonderwall' on repeat. Classic Neukölln vibe!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a U-Bahn station that doubles as a secret rave spot after hours. It's like a club buried beneath the city!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals can spot the real burrito spots by how they order: no green sauce, and definitely no 'to-go' for a sit-down meal.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing a sax in a crowded bus, next stop he gets up, finishes his solo, and everyone just goes back to scrolling their phones. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every 'bar' means tapas, but half the time they’re just serving vermut and dodgy pinchos. It’s all about the vibe, not just the food.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their hesitation at the tram stops, trying to decipher the confusing route maps while clutching a guidebook tighter than a lifebuoy.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newbie from a mile away when they try to queue politely at the bus stop. It's a free-for-all here, mate.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week, and a guy was offering up pizza slices like it was normal. Best midnight snack I've had in ages.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a cat on the U-Bahn platform waiting for the train like it owned the place. Classic Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that's also a gallery, serving up art and döner side by side. Truly a feast for the senses!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a real local by their ability to casually navigate BART delays and still make it to brunch on time, coffee in hand.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a performer on Muni with a two-foot tall parrot. Everyone on the bus was more interested in the bird than their phones.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They always think the local bars are just for tapas. Try asking for a vermut on a Sunday – that's when you'll see the real Gràcia vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they carefully avoid stepping on the cracks in the pavement, slow to realize it's just part of the game here.
